divergence: The Black Death targeted only the wealthy
headline: THE ELITE FALLEN: A PANDEMIC OF PRIVILEGE UNLEASHES CHAOS IN THE MEGACITY
deck: As the wealthy succumb to their own affluence, the underclasses rise amidst the ruins of a corporate-dominated dystopia.
article:
In the year 2087, the megacities stand as towering shadows of their former selves, draped in a digital haze that obscures the truth of this grim reality. Today, headlines scream of the latest twist in the gripping saga of the affluent elite, ravaged by a virulent strain of the Black Death, leaving them vulnerable in a world they once dominated. Wealth, once thought a shield against all perils, has become a harbinger of doom, as the virus selectively targets the affluent, leaving the impoverished masses to rise from the ashes of their dominance.

From the glimmering spires of corporate skyscrapers to the neon-soaked alleys of the undercity, the indifference of the rich has become their undoing. The outbreak has ignited a cataclysmic shift in the societal landscape, with once-subservient factions now emboldened to seize control. Armed with technology stolen from the very corporations that exploited them, the disenfranchised are etching a new narrative, one where the balance of power teeters on the edge of chaos. As the wealthy wither away, the streets thrum with revolution.

The response from the ruling class has been predictable: draconian measures enforced through stealth drones and biometric surveillance systems. Corporate enforcers patrol the streets, attempting to stifle dissent and quell the fires of rebellion. Yet, every attempt to maintain their grasp on power only serves to fuel the insurrection. The city’s underbelly, once silenced by fear, now resonates with the battle cries of those who have long been overlooked, their spirits ignited by the fall of the elite.

As we sit on the precipice of this new era, it remains to be seen whether the wealthy will fight to retain their vestiges of control or if they will surrender in the face of relentless uprisings. One thing is certain: the old dynasties are crumbling under the weight of their own arrogance, and the echoes of their demise will reverberate through the databanks of history. The last remnants of privilege may soon learn that their wealth could not insulate them from the consequences of their own hubris. As the megacity holds its breath, we stand witness to a revolution borne from a pandemic uniquely tailored to the powerful, and the world may never be the same.
author: Local Editor

oped.title: The Silver Lining of the Elite's Fall: A World Renewed
oped.author: Vera Nyx
oped.body:
In this neon-soaked metropolis of 2087, the echoes of the Great Culling, which spared the impoverished masses while claiming the affluent, still resonate. As the privileged perished, their gleaming towers fell into disrepair, morphing into homes for the downtrodden. Cybernetic advancements once hoarded by the elite are now shared among the people, reshaping our society. The digital underbelly thrives, fueled by a newfound solidarity and innovation that wealth had stifled.

Yet, as we navigate this post-plutocratic landscape, we must grapple with the ethics of our technological evolution. Are we merely replicating the hierarchies of old beneath a veneer of progress? It is imperative we build not just a new society, but a just one. In this age of neon dreams, let us harness the remnants of privilege to uplift the collective, forging a future where prosperity is defined not by wealth, but by equality.

classifieds.text: Luxury hover-seds with custom AI navigation—only 10 units left! Get yours before the next aristocrat's auction.
Seeking tech-savvy bioengineers to join the elite Surviving Society. Must have experience in pathogen resistance design.
Exclusive penthouse available in the Skyscraper District. Breathtaking views of the neon skyline, once a retreat for the wealthy.
Immediate repairs for all high-spec cyberware! Specializing in gadgets once owned by the elite—discretion guaranteed.
Lost: Neo-gem heirloom pendant in the undercity market. Reward for its safe return—no questions asked.
Wanted: Vintage tech collectors for rare auction event. Must have proof of status—let's keep the past alive!

comic.caption: Looks like the only thing rising faster than the tech prices is the death toll among the elite.
